findings.description: The North Carolina state seal image at the top of the page is not rendered in the HTML version
findings.evidence: PDF page 1 shows the Great Seal of North Carolina centered at the top. HTML page 1 does not display this seal.

findings.description: Page 1 layout differs significantly: the PDF has a centered header with the state seal, governor/secretary info on the left, and the agenda items with Roman numerals indented. The HTML version has a large blank area on page 1 with only the header info visible, and the agenda content is pushed to page 2.
findings.evidence: PDF page 1 contains the full agenda from items I through XI. HTML page 1 shows only the header/logo area with vast whitespace, and the agenda content appears on the subsequent HTML page.

findings.description: Roman numeral formatting for agenda items is slightly different - HTML uses a more compact list style without the same bold/indentation treatment
findings.evidence: PDF uses large bold Roman numerals (I., II., III., etc.) with significant indentation. HTML uses a slightly more compact format.

findings.description: The HTML rendering splits the content across more pages than the PDF due to the large blank space on page 1, pushing content down
findings.evidence: PDF has agenda on page 1 and WebEx info on page 3. HTML splits agenda across pages 2-3 and WebEx info across pages 3-4.

findings.description: The state seal image on the WebEx information page is not present in HTML
findings.evidence: PDF page 3 shows no seal on the WebEx page either, so this is consistent. The DEQ logo at the bottom is present in both versions.

findings.description: Reading order is preserved but content is distributed differently across pages due to layout differences
findings.evidence: All content items appear in the same sequence in both PDF and HTML, but pagination differs.

findings.description: The HTML has nested <main> elements and uses h2 for the WebEx section heading, which is acceptable, but the heading hierarchy includes an h3 for the meeting title on page 2 ('NC DEQ Secretaries' Science Advisory Board Meeting') which in the PDF is not subordinate to the WebEx heading — it's a parallel section label. This is a minor structural issue but not a critical one.
findings.evidence:
HTML uses

WebEx Meeting Information:

then

NC DEQ Secretaries' Science Advisory Board Meeting

. In the PDF these appear as section title and subtitle at the same level.

findings.description: The footer content (NC DEQ address and phone number) appears twice in the HTML, once per page. This mirrors the PDF which has the footer on both pages, so this is acceptable duplication matching the source.
findings.evidence: Footer with '217 West Jones Street | 1601 Mail Service Center | Raleigh, North Carolina 27699-1601' and '919-707-8700' appears twice in HTML, matching both PDF pages.

findings.description: The alt text for the two images is generic and not fully accurate. The first image alt text says 'North Carolina state seal and document background' but the PDF page 1 shows just the NC state seal at the top and the DEQ logo at the bottom. The second image says 'North Carolina DEQ document background' which is vague.
findings.evidence: First img alt='North Carolina state seal and document background'; Second img alt='North Carolina DEQ document background'. The actual images are the NC state seal and DEQ logo respectively.
